data.title: TRG International: Former Chairman's shares transferred, sale ordered by court. Unpaid award enforcement in US and Pakistan.
data.description: Legal actions are in progress in the United States and Pakistan to enforce an arbitration award due to unpaid debts. On January 19, 2026, TRG International reported that the Bermuda Supreme Court mandated the transfer of all company shares following legal proceedings. This development comes after persistent efforts to resolve the issue and emphasizes the consequences of failing to meet financial obligations. The situation highlights the importance of complying with legal decisions and the potential repercussions, with this action involving a significant amount of money.

data.title: Privacy breach under investigation at Crunchbase, Inc. over 2 million records compromised.
data.description: An investigation is currently underway by Schubert Jonckheer & Kolbe LLP regarding a data breach that took place, resulting in unauthorized entry to the personal data of individuals associated with Crunchbase, Inc., a California-based market intelligence company. The breach, which occurred on Jan. 26, 2026, has raised concerns over the exposure of sensitive information. For further information on this developing situation, please stay tuned for updates.

data.title: Micron investing $24 billion over 10 years in new Singapore wafer fab facility for long-term manufacturing and AI demands.
data.description: Over the next decade, a significant investment of around $24 billion will be made to meet the long-term manufacturing needs and cater to AI-driven demand. The allocation of this substantial sum over the specified period is aimed at bolstering manufacturing capabilities and aligning with the increasing market demand propelled by AI technology.

data.title: Runway Growth Finance Corp. starts offering unsecured notes on Nasdaq, plans for listing within 30 days.
data.description: Menlo Park, California - January 26, 2026 - Runway Growth Finance Corp. (Nasdaq: RWAY), a key provider of flexible capital solutions to late and growth-stage firms as an alternative to equity funding, has announced the initiation of an underwritten offering of unsecured notes known as the "Notes". The Company seeks to have the Notes listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market, with trading expected to start within 30 days post the original issue date, subject to market conditions. The interest rate and specific terms of the Notes will be established at the pricing of the offering.
Benchmark KOSPI climbs 1.6%, reaching an all-time high at 5,030.82.

data.title: Hackers linked to North Korea are using deepfake video calls to target crypto workers, aiming to deceive and install malware.
data.description: Cybercriminals are utilizing AI-generated technology to make video calls appear as though they are from known sources, deceiving cryptocurrency employees into downloading malicious software. This method has resulted in a significant increase in successful cyber attacks, with a notable uptick in cases involving  fraudulent video calls. The targeted workers are manipulated into unknowingly aiding hackers, leading to financial losses and data breaches. It highlights the evolving tactics of cybercriminals in exploiting advancements in technology for their illicit activities.

data.title: LG Electronics unveils total component solutions for commercial rooftop systems at AHR Expo 2026, showcasing HVAC solutions in Las Vegas.
data.description: LG Electronics, a leading manufacturer of core components for commercial outdoor units, is emphasizing efficiency, flexibility, and simplified service at the AHR Expo 2026 in Las Vegas. The company is showcasing comprehensive HVAC component solutions for commercial rooftop systems. LG's focus on these key aspects aims to enhance performance and convenience for users. This exhibition highlights LG's commitment to innovation in the HVAC industry.

data.title: Cryptocurrencies slightly up: Bitcoin $88,316.74 (+0.93%), Ethereum $2,924.02 (+1.80%), XRP $1.90 (+1.14%), Dogecoin $0.1222 (+0.03%). Bitcoin trading volume up 10% in 24 hours, $360M liquidated in market. Market sentiment improving, open interest fell 0.51%.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies and stocks saw gains on Monday, with a reversal in the precious metal rally as investors embraced higher risk. Notable crypto 24-hour gains included Bitcoin at +0.93% reaching $88,316.74, Ethereum at +1.80% hitting $2,924.02, XRP at +1.14% reaching $1.90, Solana at +2.01% hitting $124.09, and Dogecoin at +0.03% reaching $0.1222. Bitcoin rose to an intraday high of 88,743, with trading volume up by 10%. Ethereum almost broke $3,000, and XRP also saw gains. Bitcoin's dominance was at 59%, with Ethereum holding nearly 12% of the market share. Over $360 million was liquidated in the past 24 hours, with $265 million in bearish short positions. Bitcoin's open interest declined by 0.51%, indicating a short covering trend where short sellers buy back their positions. This uptrend in prices coupled with a drop in open interest suggests improved market sentiment.
